title: Charred Indulgent Egyptian Almonds Baklava
description: A rich and creamy Egyptian dessert featuring amaretti, almonds, and a sweet honey syrup. Suitable for Buddhist temple-friendly gatherings.
instructions: Make a water bath by adding 1 cup water to a medium saucepan and bringing to a boil.
In a small bowl, sprinkle gelatin over 1/4 cup cold water and let bloom for 5 minutes.
Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add almonds to skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly charred, about 5 minutes.
Stir in sugar, vanilla extract, and salt. Cook for 1 minute.
Add honey to skillet and stir to combine. Cook for 2 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together cream cheese and amaretti cookies until smooth.
Add gelatin mixture to cream cheese mixture and mix until well combined.
Press 1/3 of the almond mixture into the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Spread half of the cream cheese mixture over the almonds.
Repeat layers, ending with cream cheese mixture on top.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours or overnight.
